LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FANYA YOUNG'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT TEGSCO, LLC FOR VIOLATING CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 5.125 AND 5.1312 IS DENIED BECAUSE IT WAS UNTIMELY FILED AND THE RULES CITED IN IT PROVIDE NO BASIS FOR THE SANCTIONS REQUESTED. DEFENDANTS' CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT WAS PROPER AND THE COURT'S SUGGESTION OF A MEDIATION WAS NOT A REQUIREMENT. PREVAILING/MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND DENYING NON-PARTY TEGSCO LLC'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ORDERED RESTRAINING DEFENDANTS FROM TRANSFERRING RIGHT, TITLE OR INTEREST IN THE 2009 NISSAN SENTRA PENDING TRIAL IN THIS ACTION IS HEARD. PLAINTIFF FANYA YOUNG'S APPLICATION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. MS. YOUNG HAS NOT SHOWN THAT ANY OF THE DEFENDANTS ARE IN POSSESSION OF HER VEHICLE OR THAT ANY OF THE DEFENDANTS ARE IN A POSITION TO COMPLY WITH AN ORDER ENJOINING THE TRANSFER OF THE VEHICLE. NON-PARTY TEGSCO LLC'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING THAT MS. YOUNG'S APPLICATION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WAS FILED FOR THE PURPOSES OF DELAY OR THAT IT HAD NO CONCEIVABLE BASIS TO SUPPORT IT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR DELAY IN DISCHARGING OF THE TEMPORARY RETRAINING ORDER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS INSTRUCTED TO SEEK TO AMEND COMPLAINT AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

THE PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO MEET AND CONFER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41 NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 13, 2017. IF THOSE MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS DO NOT RESOLVE ALL THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER, PLAINTIFF FANYA YOUNG HAS UNTIL NOVEMBER 27, 2017 TO FILE AND SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS AND THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS HAVE UNTIL DECEMBER 4, 2017 TO FILE AND SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Continued To Dec-11-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fanya Young (415-966-9684) appears as self-represented plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS JOHN (AN INDIVIDUAL) WICKER, TEGSCO LLC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Continued to December 11, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer. The parties are required to meet and confer in compliance with CCP 430.41 no later than November 13, 2017. If those meet and confer efforts do not resolve all the issues raised by the demurrer, plaintiff Fanya Young has until November 27, 2017 to file and serve a supplemental opposition papers and the demurring defendants have until December 4, 2017 to file and serve supplemental reply papers.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Tegsco, Llc For Violating Cal. Rules Of Court 5.125, 5.1312
Off Calendar Jul-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jodie Smith, Esq. (415-362-3599) of Moscone Emblidge & Otis for Defendant Tegsco LLC.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FANYA YOUNG'S Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Tegsco, Llc For Violating California Rules Of Court 5.125 and 5.1312 is DENIED because it was untimely filed and the rules cited in it provide no basis for the sanctions requested. Defendants' case management statement was proper and the Court's suggestion of a mediation was not a requirement.
Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2017 Continued To Aug-30-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - FANYA YOUNG, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JODIE SMITH OF THE MOSCONE EMBLIDGE & OTIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Tegsco LLC's demurrer to the third through eighth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the third through seventh causes of action and twenty days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action. The Fair Debt Collection Practices Act claims are not viable for towing charges. Plaintiff Fanya Young acknowledges that her fraud claim is deficient. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Young is given leave to file a second amended complaint alleging any cognizable claims she has against Tegsco including fraud but not including the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Ordered Restraining Defendants From Transferring Right, Title Or Interest In The 2009 Nissan Sentra Pending Trial In This Action
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fanya Young (415.650.9791 ) appears as counsel for plaintiff/applicant

Attorney, Jodie Smith of the law offices of Moscone Emblidge & Otis LLP (415.362.3599) appears as counsel for non-party, Tegsco LLC

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Order To Show Cause Why A Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Ordered Restraining Defendants From Transferring Right, Title Or Interest In The 2009 Nissan Sentra Pending Trial In This Action is heard. Plaintiff Fanya Young's application for a preliminary injunction is DENIED. Ms. Young has not shown that any of the defendants are in possession of her vehicle or that any of the defendants are in a position to comply with an order enjoining the transfer of the vehicle. Non-party Tegsco LLC's request for sanctions is denied. There has been no showing that Ms. Young's application for a preliminary injunction was filed for the purposes of delay or that it had no conceivable basis to support it. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Plaintiff's request for delay in discharging of the temporary retraining order is denied. Plaintiff is instructed to seek to amend complaint as soon as possible.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
